commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=8c204f384ccf5abd7dc1a7fe129f5c68cf5f55a9)
commands.review_summary:
The PR extends Telegram polling-session failure handling to dead-letter `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` and adds a three-strike poison retry counter with regression tests.

PR surface: Source +122, Tests +204. Total +326 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main sends unclassified `bot.handleUpdate` failures through `releaseTelegramSpooledUpdateClaim`, while the classifier lacks `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND`. I did not run a live Telegram reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] External contributor proof is mock-only and the remaining code blocker changes Telegram delivery policy, so this needs contributor or maintainer follow-up rather than a ClawSweeper repair marker.

target.title: fix(telegram): dead-letter ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND and poison-retry spooled updates (#92980)
target.body:
## Summary

Telegram spooled updates that hit a permanent error were released for retry, picked up again on the same drain, and looped until the gateway became unresponsive. Issue [#92980](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/92980) reports a ~10 minute freeze caused by `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` on a stale bundled JS chunk and an unknown agent harness, both classified as retryable despite never resolving on retry.

This PR fixes #92980 by extending the Telegram-owned non-retryable failure policy and adding a poison-retry backstop:

- `resolveNonRetryableSpooledUpdateFailure` now also classifies `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` (anywhere in the `cause` / `error` graph) as a new `module-not-found` non-retryable reason, dead-lettering the update on the first failure. The classification runs before the missing-harness check so a wrapped cause is not masked by an outer retryable error.
- A new in-memory poison counter keys by `updateId`, tracks consecutive same-signature failures, and dead-letters the update as `poison-retry-exceeded` after 3 consecutive hits. The cap is bounded (`TELEGRAM_SPOOLED_UPDATE_POISON_RETRY_LIMIT = 3`) so any future classification gap fails closed instead of looping the gateway. Successful processing clears the counter, and dead-lettered non-retryable paths clear it as well.
- The signature is `extractErrorCode(err) ?? readErrorName(err) ?? "unknown"` collected from the error graph, so the same transient network blip groups together but unrelated failures split.

No new config surface. Per ClawSweeper review on #92980, the env-var retry limit is intentionally not added — `openclaw.json` is already large (root `AGENTS.md`: "Config/env surface bar is high") and a fixed default is the safer first implementation. A future maintainer can promote it to config if real-world traffic proves 3 too low.

## Behavior

- `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` from `bot.handleUpdate` (direct, `{ cause }` wrapped, grammY `BotError` wrapped) → dead-lettered on first occurrence, same lane drains normally.
- Unknown / unclassified failure that repeats 3 times in a row with the same signature → dead-lettered as `poison-retry-exceeded`. Different signatures reset the counter, so a transient network blip that turns into a real bug still gets triaged.
- Successful processing of an update → counter cleared. The next failure of that update starts at 1, not at the historical run.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/telegram-ingress-spool.test.ts` — **71 passed (8 new tests)**
  - `dead-letters ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND failures so later same-lane updates can drain` (direct)
  - `dead-letters cause-wrapped ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND failures` (`{ cause }`)
  - `dead-letters grammY BotError-wrapped ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND failures` (`BotError.error`)
  - `dead-letters unknown retryable failures after the poison retry cap is reached` (3× poison, dead-letter)
  - `resets the poison counter once the update finally succeeds` (counter cleared on success)
  - The 3 existing missing-harness dead-letter tests still pass.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json` — clean
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts` — clean
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts` — clean
- `git diff --check` — clean

## What was not tested

- No live Telegram gateway reproduction. The fix rests on source-level proof of the retry path (`polling-session.ts:releaseFailedSpooledUpdate` → `releaseTelegramSpooledUpdateClaim`) plus the test matrix that exhaustively covers the classifier. The reporter's concrete production log narrative (13:24:56–13:34:52 UTC loop, ~10 minutes frozen) is the real-world signal that backs the failure mode.
- The signature grouping assumes `extractErrorCode` is meaningful for the failure modes we care about (errno-style codes + error names). A future failure mode that throws a bare `Error("message")` with no name and no code groups under the default `unknown` signature, which is the intended collapse for opaque errors.

## Related

- Closes #92980
- Supersedes the in-flight narrower fix #93014 (Coder-Wangyankun), which only covered `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` and did not address the repeated same-signature poison case called out in the issue's proposed fix #3.
- Reviewer cross-references: `extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.ts:144` (classifier), `extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.ts:784` (release path), `extensions/telegram/src/telegram-ingress-spool.ts:283` (`failTelegramSpooledUpdateClaim` primitive).

## Real environment tested

Local dev checkout, Node 22.19+, pnpm-managed. No live Telegram bot provisioned for this change; the harness path is covered by unit tests using `startIsolatedIngressSession`.
target.author: nianjiuzst
